title: Hearty Syrian Veggies Stir-fry
description: A savory and gooey Syrian dish featuring diabetic-friendly vegetables like celery, carrots, and onions in a tangy tomato sauce with a hint of cumin.

ingredients.name: Carrots
ingredients.notes: use baby carrots for easier chopping and cooking

ingredients.name: Celery
ingredients.notes: cut into thin slices or dice finely

ingredients.name: Water/Soy Milk (optional)
ingredients.notes: for thinning the sauce if needed

instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add onions and minced garlic to sauté until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add diced carrots and celery slices. Cook for an additional 3-4 minutes or until vegetables are slightly tender.
Stir in cumin seeds and cook for another minute.
Pour in chopped tomatoes (or tomato sauce if using), water/soy milk, salt and pepper to taste.
Simmer on low heat for about 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, to allow flavors to meld together.
Garnish with fresh parsley before serving.
